Based on the provided context, Robert M. Fomon's connection to the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) was that he served as a **governor** of the exchange.

The text identifies the NYSE as being located at 11 Wall Street in Lower Manhattan and separately notes that Robert M. Fomon was a "governor of the New York Stock Exchange" during his career as a financier and CEO of E. F. Hutton & Co.
